course_title: Language Awareness & Cross-Cultural Competence for the Global Workplace

learning_outcomes.description: Recognise the ways in which language in communication is used to build relationships and trust, and to exclude and marginalise.
Effectively apply verbal and non-verbal language and communication knowledge and skills to intercultural communication contexts
Effectively apply theories and concepts in analysing communication, identify problems and provide solutions.

Critically reflect upon your view of communication and its influence on interaction to demonstrate your adaptability and responsibility as an effective communicator in increasingly globalised and culturally diverse workplaces.

assessments.due_date:
25/08/2026

Second half of the tutorial in WEEK 5
assessments.task_description:
This is a paper-based exam that will be made up of: 

  • Multiple choice questions;
  • Open-ended questions (readings + content)
  • Practical tasks or Data analysis relating to content from Module 1.

This will be marked out 15 marks.

The exam will take place in the second half of the tutorial in WEEK 5.

This is an open book exam.

STATEMENT ON GEN AI & MT

This assessment is designed to protect academic integrity and assess your understanding of course concepts. You must only use the approved materials and tools for this assessment task. Use of prohibited materials in this task may be investigated and treated as academic misconduct. See assessment instructions for more details.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.

assessments.task_description:
Students will record a vlog in Week 12. In the vlog post, you will respond in 5 minutes to a set of questions about the course material in the three modules. Questions will be provided via Blackboard in Week 11. You will be asked to explain key concepts in the material and relate them to your experience and/or views. You will be required to upload your vlog in Blackboard.

STATEMENT ON GEN AI & MT

In this assessment you can use resources such as your notes, course materials, and other tools, including AI, while completing the task. The focus is not on memorising information, but on understanding, applying judgement, and demonstrating your own thinking.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.due_date:
27/10/2026

This data analysis presentation will occur in person in WEEK 13 tutorial.
assessments.task_description:
In the Week 13 tutorial, each student will be given examples of real workplace communication. Students are required to analyse the interaction between participants, identify key issues and topics, and provide informed recommendations based on concepts and frameworks studied in COMU2040.

Each student will have:

  • 5 minutes to read the provided material and make notes
  • 5 minutes to present their analysis

 Students will be assessed individually based on the following criteria:

  1. Application of course concepts
  2. Demonstrates the ability to analyse workplace communication using relevant theories, concepts, and tools covered in the course.
  3. Identification of communication issues
  4. Identifies and critically evaluates problems or breakdowns in the interaction within a workplace communication context.
  5. Quality of recommendations
  6. Provides clear, relevant, and practical recommendations informed by course content and tailored to the specific workplace context presented in the example.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.
